{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether nintedanib holds promise for lung disease associated with systemic sclerosis. Multiple documents confirm that nintedanib is specifically indicated for systemic sclerosis-associated interstitial lung disease (SSc-ILD). Document [2] states that nintedanib was approved by the FDA in 2019 for managing interstitial lung disease in systemic sclerosis, with clinical trials showing a slowing of the decline in pulmonary function tests. Document [3] notes that nintedanib demonstrated significant slowing of fibrosis in SSc-ILD and slowed the decline of forced vital capacity (FVC). Document [4] confirms that nintedanib is indicated for treating SSc-ILD. Document [5] states that nintedanib effectively slows disease progression and potentially reduces all-cause mortality in patients with SSc-ILD. Document [6] further supports this by noting that the American Thoracic Society (ATS) has issued a conditional recommendation for nintedanib in SSc-ILD. These consistent findings across multiple sources indicate that nintedanib has been clinically validated for use in systemic sclerosis-associated lung disease. Therefore, it clearly holds promise.", "answer_choice": "A"}
